text.tspan: Nameplate Components display visual information for specified entities during stage runtime, including
entity HP, level, directional guidance, distance display, and other visual indicators.
Supported entity types: Characters, Objects, and Creations
- Multiple nameplates can be active simultaneously
- Controlled via default config, node graph, or local filter for visibility

svg.text: On the same physics layer as the entity
In entity/prefab editing interface, open the Editing Components tab
Click "Advanced Editing" to expand the editing tab
Dropdown enumeration; checkbox toggles effective status (synced to editing tab)
Enumerates all nameplates configured for the entity
Preset or custom attachment point; nameplate position uses this as base
Effective range; nameplate logic runs only within this distance
Local filter to determine visibility on local client
Boolean: visible when "true" | Integer: visible when within effective integer range
Enable simultaneously with object creation (may cause overlapping)

text.tspan: Text exceeding box range will not be displayed

svg.text: s: non-character | a: character | p: player

text.tspan: Percentage | No Text | Current Value | 1:1 Scale
Do Not Show | Progress Bar | Progress Bar + Value (2 decimal places)
Do Not Show | Display Separately (shield bar below HP) | Mixed Display (combined bar)
Shield value right, HP value left, according to deduction priority
When checked, shield bar displays as full if exceeding configured max
Limit value; deduction shown only when shield drops below this
Smooth transition between nameplate and indicator

svg.text: Size, Background Color, Font Size, Text (variable insertion)
Size, Icon Type: Custom Icon or Use Faction Icon

text.tspan: Output is the entity with nameplate component mounted
Output is the character entity within effective range (succeeds only if local client character)

svg.text: Target Entity: Runtime entity | Nameplate Config ID List: Completely overrides active nameplates

text.tspan: 1. Create NPC prefab, add Nameplate component + Custom Attachment Point component
2. Add custom attachment point (set Name, Offset, Scale as needed)
3. Add nameplate, configure Attachment Point, Visible Radius, Type, Navigation Indicator
4. Nameplate content: Text Box (quest info) + Icon
5. Enable Navigation Indicator: set Max/Min Visible Radius
6. Nav indicator content: Text Box (distance via Insert Variable - PDist) + Icon
7. Place prefab in scene and enter Test Mode
Combined Usage: Nameplate + Speech Bubble on same NPC for interaction-triggered dialogue

svg.text: - Nameplate ID (e.g., "Nameplate X") can be used as node input to adjust activation parameters.
- Use Custom Attachment Points for precise nameplate positioning above entity heads.

a.text: Miliastra Academy Documentation: Nameplates and Speech Bubbles Tutorial

svg.text: AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
